name: wasabi-stats-api
description: Reads Wasabi account and bucket storage statistics from the Wasabi Stats API.

risk.read: external Wasabi Stats API read of account/bucket storage usage metrics
risk.approval: none; read-only, no reverse-ETL write surface

conformance.reason: AUTH_COMPLEX (quarantine.json): legacy's requester() branches auth mode on the CONTENTS of the api_key secret at runtime -- Bearer api_key_header unless strings.SplitN(key, ":", 2) yields exactly 2 parts, in which case Basic(parts[0], parts[1]) is used instead -- which the engine's `when` grammar (equality/membership/truthiness over a whole resolved value, conventions.md Sec3) cannot express, since it has no string-split/contains primitive to branch on a SUBSTRING of a secret's value. hook-covered by hooks/wasabi-stats-api/hooks.go's AuthHook, proven by hooks_test.go and mirroring gmail's identical custom-auth-only skip_dynamic precedent: conformance's synthetic secret value can exercise only one of the two legacy-equivalent branches at a time, so every auth-resolving dynamic check would otherwise pass or fail arbitrarily depending on which branch conformance's fixed synthetic value happens to hit, not exercise both
